rationale: CODE EXISTS BUT DOES THE WRONG THING (caught BY EXECUTION, not text): the spec COMMITS to runtime_emit_event_class='build_runner.governance.lockstep.verified'. The build-runner embodiment IS active in the 'build_runner.*' namespace (it really emits build_runner.session.start / build_runner.dispatch.received / build_runner.session.end / build_runner.build.phase.start etc. into outputs/build_runner_events.jsonl) -- but it NEVER emits the COMMITTED class 'build_runner.governance.lockstep.verified'. The probe reads the real emission record, sees same-namespace activity but NO committed-class emission, and returns fidelity_ok=false via the EXECUTION path (disposition=not_faithful, judge_invoked=false). A name-only / registry checker would WRONGLY pass this because the spec declares the class; the execution-grounded probe catches that the code does the WRONG thing. This is the Done #32 behavioral-fidelity-bar / KT-8 negative test.
